subject: Re:
body:
i'm told than the taxi dock was never an option ,, it is too crowded and too shallow


----- Original Message ----
From: John Amerling <john_amerling@earthlink.net>
To: J. Epstein <jeeproject@yahoo.com>
Cc: DKIESQ@aol.com
Sent: Monday, May 21, 2007 7:28:56 AM
Subject: 


According to discussions with Steve over the last 24 hours, we can keep the new barge at Crown Bay until November at the rate of not more than $90 a day (monthly rate might be lower). A safe harbor for storms could be provided by Steve in the lagoon on Water Island next to his barge. I went over what he would do in a storm and it seemed reasonable. As suggested by Darren I discussed possible contract terms with Steve and he is to give me a simple proposal today. We mutually pledged to have a contract in place by the time he got back here by communicating by phone and e mail. So, we have a solution in hand for berthing for a time period which allows us to pursue a longer term plan and I have embarked upon a program to get a fair contract with Steve. I assume that I have the authority to conduct the negotiations and to present the results to you and to Darren. In this role I will confirm independently the price and terms of berthing at Crown Bay directly with the owners of
 the property. I will pursue the Rampino contract to conclusion to obtain written contracts for both berthing and with Steve which meet with your approval. Please let me know if I am not on the right track here. 
 
Lafayette Contractors LLC
6100 Red Hook Quarter, Suite A2-7
St. Thomas, VI 00802
Tel: 340 779-8054
Fax:340 779-8055
Cell:340 690-7194
